howItWorks.intro: This net worth calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Net Worth = Total Assets - Total Liabilities
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What counts as an asset?
faq.answer: Cash, savings, investments (stocks, bonds, retirement accounts), real estate, vehicles, jewelry, and other valuables. Use current market value, not what you paid.

faq.question: What counts as a liability?
faq.answer: All debts: mortgage, car loans, student loans, credit card balances, personal loans, and any other money owed.

faq.question: What is a good net worth by age?
faq.answer: A common benchmark: by 30 aim for 1x salary saved, by 40 aim for 3x, by 50 aim for 6x, by 60 aim for 8x, and by 67 aim for 10x your salary.

faq.question: Should I include my car?
faq.answer: Include it at current resale value (not what you paid). Cars depreciate quickly, so this value drops over time.

howWeCalculate.intro: This net worth calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs
Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This net worth calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.
scenarios: When planning major financial decisions like buying a home, investing, or taking on debt
When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side
When building a budget or projecting future savings and expenses
When you want to verify a quote or estimate from a financial advisor or lender
When teaching or learning about personal finance and compound growth
